foundations.name: Allostatic Load Accumulation
foundations.statement: Chronic unresolved activation accumulates as allostatic load.
foundations.scope_notes: Much wear reverses with environmental correction. Some may not (telomere shortening, hippocampal volume loss).

foundations.name: Market Proxy Exploitation
foundations.statement: Markets can industrialize proxy exploitation of unmet regulatory needs.
foundations.derivation: P3 + DA4 + market incentive structures + empirical (Moss food, Schull gambling, Haugen attention, Muldoon & Park companion AI).

foundations.name: Environment as Primary Intervention Layer
foundations.statement: Cortical override of subcortical activation is metabolically expensive, and chronic defensive activation downregulates the cortical override machinery itself.
foundations.derivation: Empirical corollary of DA8 + DA9; supported by Forest Troop, Roseto, Ilardi TLC, BEIP, captive-chimpanzee enrichment.
foundations.scope_notes: Interventions that change the organism's actual input conditions are therefore frequently more durable and less effort-dependent than interventions targeting cognitive override alone. The environment is a primary intervention layer for systemic and population-scale change, and is currently underweighted by major institutions. This does not preclude individual-level cognitive, pharmacological, or psychotherapeutic interventions where indicated; the claim is about default leverage and systemic design, not exclusivity or head-to-head comparative efficacy in individual cases.
additional_paragraphs: DC3 reframes intervention strategy at the systemic and population-scale level. Cortical override is metabolically costly and is degraded by exactly the chronic activation states that demand it most, so designing environments that do not require constant override is typically higher leverage than scaling up override capacity. This is not a claim that cognitive, pharmacological, or psychotherapeutic interventions fail - they often work, and where indicated they should be used. It is a claim about which layer institutional and design decisions should default to when both layers are available, particularly for population-scale outcomes. Whether environmental correction outperforms user-side intervention in head-to-head comparisons is an empirical question Cor does not currently make a strong claim on; the leverage claim is the primary content of DC3.

foundations.name: Fitness Interface
foundations.statement: Access to the world is mediated by evolved fitness interfaces, not guaranteed veridicality.
foundations.derivation: Hoffman (Interface Theory of Perception), Prakash et al. (Fitness Beats Truth theorem).
foundations.scope_notes: Everything experienced is a species-specific interface shaped by natural selection to track fitness payoffs, not truth. The specification operates entirely within this interface. OF1 does not enter the derivation chain - its function is to clarify that every mechanism output is a fitness-relevant signal by design, and to keep the door open to ontological questions outside the interface.

foundations.name: Signal-Default Epistemology
foundations.statement: Motivational-emotional outputs are treated as prima facie informative about the organism's regulated conditions, by default.
foundations.derivation: Frame; the diagnostic default the atlas applies before any clinical or design intervention.
foundations.scope_notes: The signal is read as information about input conditions and current state, not as noise to be suppressed. This is a diagnostic default, not a presumption of veridicality (OF1 stands) and not a contraindication to symptom relief where indicated. The default is displaced by positive evidence of decoupling or direct physiological perturbation: developmental miscalibration carried forward, chronic dysregulation, acute organic disease or state shift (sleep deprivation, inflammation, endocrine perturbation, delirium), structural damage, and substance or medication effects.

foundations.name: Inclusive Fitness
foundations.statement: Inclusive fitness - survival and reproduction - is the loss function.
foundations.scope_notes: The architecture exists because it contributed to the survival and reproduction of the organism and its genetic relatives across evolutionary time. The loss function explains why the mechanisms exist (ultimate). The mechanisms are how the loss function is implemented (proximate). No organism computes inclusive fitness explicitly - the mechanisms are proximate implementations shaped by selection. This is why proxies fail: they trigger input cues while failing to supply resolution conditions.

foundations.name: Domain-Sensitive Interacting Adaptations
foundations.statement: The organism contains evolved, domain-sensitive, interacting functional adaptations.
foundations.derivation: Tooby & Cosmides (design principle), confirmed empirically by Panksepp, Bowlby, Dunbar, Boehm, Trivers, Gilbert, Rozin.
foundations.scope_notes: "Organism" not "mind" - the immune system, HPA axis, circadian biology, gut-brain axis, thermoregulation are all evolved functional adaptations with environmental requirements. "Interacting" not "modular" - dense cross-system connections disprove informational encapsulation.

foundations.name: Systematic Mismatch
foundations.statement: Modern environments often push these adaptations outside their expected or regulatable operating ranges.
foundations.derivation: Eaton & Konner (discordance), Gluckman & Hanson (developmental mismatch), Li/van Vugt/Colarelli (mismatch review). Constrained by Boyd & Richerson / Henrich.
foundations.scope_notes:
Three modes - deprivation (expected inputs missing), conflict/overload (incompatible simultaneous demands), proxy hijack (false cues overstimulating systems). "Matched" means within the expectable range, often culturally scaffolded - NOT ancestrally identical in surface form. Modernity is mixed - it also brought sanitation, reduced violence, analgesia, literacy. The spec is diagnostic, not blanket indictment.

[epistemic_grade full phrasing from handoff: forced (existence) / strongly_supported (scope). Stored primary label: forced - the existence of systematic mismatch is forced by P2+P3; the scope is strongly_supported.]

foundations.name: Defensive Signals Under Mismatch
foundations.statement: Many aversive outputs are intelligible defensive signals under mismatch, not evidence of defective design.
foundations.derivation: P2 + P3 + Nesse (negative outputs as defenses) + McEwen (reversible with environmental correction) + Birkett & Newton-Fisher (100% captive chimps abnormal, 0% wild).
foundations.scope_notes:
Does NOT claim all psychiatric presentations are mismatch signals. Four categories must be distinguished: defensive activation, dysregulation, damage, developmental miscalibration.

[epistemic_grade full phrasing from handoff: forced (qualitative) / strongly_supported (quantitative). Stored primary label: forced - the qualitative claim (defensive signal, not defect) is forced by derivation; quantitative extent is strongly_supported.]

foundations.name: Socially Scaffolded Regulation
foundations.statement: Human regulation is constitutively socially scaffolded; social context is likely the highest-leverage cross-cutting input dimension.
foundations.derivation: Dunbar + Coan & Sbarra (social baseline) + Bowlby (attachment) + Hrdy (cooperative breeding).
foundations.scope_notes: [epistemic_grade full phrasing from handoff: forced (modulation) / strongly_supported (primacy). Stored primary label: forced - social modulation of regulation is forced; that social is THE highest-leverage dimension is strongly_supported.]

foundations.name: Recurrent Coupling and Cascading
foundations.statement: Systems are recurrently coupled; perturbations propagate and can self-maintain across domains.
foundations.derivation: Panksepp (cross-system inhibition) + Felitti (ACE dose-response) + Borsboom (network psychopathology) + McEwen (cumulative cross-system activation).

foundations.name: Proxy Hijacking via Open Loops
foundations.statement: Proxy cues can activate systems without meeting the conditions that normally regulate or terminate them.
foundations.scope_notes: Not all proxies fail equally. Resolution conditions must be independently specified, not inferred backward from proxy failure.

foundations.name: Defensive Over-Activation Bias
foundations.statement: Defensive systems err toward over-activation under asymmetric error costs.
foundations.derivation: Haselton & Buss (error management) + Nesse (smoke detector) + P1.
foundations.scope_notes: [epistemic_grade qualifier from handoff: forced (for defensive systems).]

foundations.name: Competing Motivational Programs
foundations.statement: The architecture contains partially competing motivational programs and tradeoff structures.
foundations.derivation: Trivers (parent-offspring conflict, reciprocal altruism) + Boehm (dominance + anti-dominance) + Gilbert (approach + submit).
foundations.scope_notes: "Matched" does NOT mean "conflict-free." Matched vs mismatched = managed vs unmanaged tension.

foundations.name: Developmental Calibration
foundations.statement: The architecture calibrates to developmental and ongoing environmental input within evolved ranges.
foundations.derivation: Bowlby (internal working models) + Hrdy + McEwen + Meaney (HPA-axis calibration).
foundations.scope_notes: BROAD life history theory rejected. NARROW architectural calibration (specific systems calibrate during specific windows) is forced.

foundations.name: Phylogenetic Priority
foundations.statement: Motivational programs have phylogenetically determined priority relations. Older, survival-critical systems can suppress or commandeer newer systems.
foundations.derivation: Panksepp (phylogenetic depth of affective circuits) + cross-species conservation evidence.

foundations.name: Non-Substitutability of Mechanism Resolution
foundations.statement: Mechanism resolution conditions are not fully interchangeable. Improvement in one mechanism can buffer or modulate consequences in another, especially where mechanisms are recurrently coupled (DA3), but cross-system buffering does not generally replace the specific resolution conditions of an impaired mechanism.
foundations.derivation: Bottleneck heuristic from Liebig 1840; follows from P2; coupling effects handled by DA3.
foundations.scope_notes: Well-being is often bottlenecked by severe unresolved deficits in individual mechanisms and is not well described as a simple average across mechanism states.
additional_paragraphs: DA9 distinguishes the architecture from utility-aggregation frameworks. A severely degraded M3 cannot be substituted by a flourishing M2 - the resolution conditions are not interchangeable, even when the mechanisms are coupled (DA3) and one can buffer or modulate the other. The protocol therefore measures mechanism dimensions independently rather than collapsing them into a single well-being index. Liebig's Law of the Minimum (1840) is the historical heuristic for the bottleneck pattern: plant growth is often constrained by the scarcest essential nutrient. Cor adopts the bottleneck framing, not a strict biochemical claim of zero substitutability - the human architecture does show cross-system buffering, and DA3 handles that coupling explicitly.

convergences.name: Inclusive Fitness as Loss Function
convergences.statement: Natural selection operates on inclusive fitness - survival plus reproduction weighted by relatedness - establishing a single loss function that all evolved motivational architecture serves, as demonstrated independently by Darwinian selection theory, Hamilton's kin-selection formalism, and experimental altruistic-punishment findings that humans enforce cooperation at personal cost where inclusive-fitness logic predicts.
independent_literatures: Fehr & Gächter experimental economics of altruistic punishment

convergences.name: Threat-Detection via Ancient Subcortical Circuits
convergences.statement: Threat detection and defensive response are mediated by phylogenetically ancient subcortical circuits (amygdala, periaqueductal gray, hypothalamus) that operate pre-attentively and can override cortical processing - established independently by Panksepp's FEAR-system neuroanatomy, the Öhman/Mineka backward-masking fear-module research program, and Nesse's clinical evolutionary psychiatry linking panic and anxiety to these defensive systems.
independent_literatures: Öhman & Mineka fear module visual attention / backward masking
Nesse evolutionary psychiatry of panic and anxiety

convergences.name: Reciprocity, Norm Enforcement, and Coalition Architecture
convergences.statement: Humans have specialized architecture for reciprocal exchange, cheater detection, norm enforcement, and coalitional identity - supported independently by Fehr & Gächter's experimental altruistic-punishment findings, Boehm's reverse-dominance coalition dynamics in hunter-gatherer anthropology, and Tomasello's shared-intentionality comparative-cognition research.
independent_literatures: Fehr & Gächter experimental economics of cooperation
Tomasello comparative cognition and shared intentionality

convergences.name: Developmental Calibration within Evolved Ranges
convergences.statement: Specific physiological and behavioral systems calibrate during specific developmental windows to environmental inputs within evolved parameters; inputs outside those parameters produce persistent miscalibration - established independently by Bowlby's attachment developmental calibration, McEwen's HPA-axis calibration neuroendocrinology, and Felitti's ACE Study dose-response evidence for durable effects of early-life exposure.
independent_literatures: Felitti ACE Study clinical developmental epidemiology

convergences.name: Aversive Outputs as Intelligible Defensive Signals
convergences.statement: Many aversive psychological and physiological outputs (anxiety, low mood, pain, grief, fatigue, anger) are intelligible defensive signals produced by architecture that is functioning correctly in response to inputs outside its expected operating range - established independently by Nesse's 'good reasons for bad feelings' evolutionary psychiatry, Panksepp's affective neuroscience of conserved aversive systems, and Fehr & Gächter's finding that anger/moralistic outrage is the proximate mechanism for prosocial norm enforcement rather than a dysregulation.
independent_literatures: Nesse evolutionary psychiatry of defensive signals
Panksepp affective neuroscience of aversive systems
Fehr & Gächter experimental economics of moralistic outrage

convergences.name: Reproductive Motivation as Distinct Architecture
convergences.statement: Reproductive motivation (desire, attraction, pair-bonding) is a distinct architectural system independent of general-purpose pursuit, with sex-differentiated substrates and a characteristic proxy gradient from real partner to pure proxy - established independently by Panksepp's LUST-circuit affective neuroscience, Buss's sexual strategies theory and sex-differentiated mate preferences research, and Haselton/Buss's error-management prediction of sex-differentiated perceptual biases around mating cues.
independent_literatures: Haselton & Buss error management in cross-sex mind reading

convergences.name: Domain-Sensitive Organism Architecture
convergences.statement: The human organism contains evolved, domain-sensitive, interacting functional adaptations rather than a general-purpose learner - supported independently by Tooby & Cosmides' design-principle derivation, Panksepp's conserved subcortical affective-motivational systems, and Bowlby's attachment-as-primary-motivational-system framework.
independent_literatures: Tooby & Cosmides evolutionary psychology design principle

convergences.name: Systematic EEA-Modern Environment Mismatch
convergences.statement: Modern post-agricultural, post-industrial environments push evolved adaptations outside their expected operating ranges in systematic and predictable ways - established independently by Eaton & Konner's discordance hypothesis, Li/van Vugt/Colarelli's mismatch-identification framework, Boyd & Richerson's gene-culture coevolution constraint (matched must include cultural scaffolding), and Henrich's WEIRD empirical diagnostic showing Western populations anchor the extreme end of the cross-cultural distribution.
independent_literatures: Eaton & Konner evolutionary medicine / discordance hypothesis
Li van Vugt Colarelli mismatch hypothesis review

convergences.name: Phylogenetic Conservation of Subcortical Affective Systems
convergences.statement: The core affective-motivational systems (SEEKING, FEAR, RAGE, CARE, PANIC/GRIEF, PLAY, LUST) are anatomically and functionally conserved across mammals and extend into birds for specific systems - established independently by Panksepp's deep-brain-stimulation and cross-species neuroscience, Bowlby's attachment cross-mammalian homology, and Hrdy's cross-primate parental/alloparental evidence.

convergences.name: Socially Scaffolded Regulation via Attachment
convergences.statement: Human regulation is constitutively socially scaffolded: the organism's default expectation is proximity to trusted co-regulators, and this scaffolding is architectural rather than optional - established independently by Coan's Social Baseline Theory fMRI program, Bowlby's attachment theory, Hrdy's cooperative-breeding thesis, Dunbar's social-brain hypothesis, and Holt-Lunstad's mortality meta-analysis showing 50% survival advantage from strong social relationships.
independent_literatures: Coan social baseline theory affective neuroscience
additional_paragraphs: The 150 parameter has direct empirical validation: Facebook friend-count studies on UK national samples (N≈2000 and N≈1375) returned mean friend counts of ~155 and ~183, statistically indistinguishable from the prediction, with only ~14% of users exceeding 300 friends (Dunbar 2016).

Three of these literatures explicitly engage Dunbar's parameter in their own frameworks: Tomasello and Hrdy build on his layered social architecture directly (shared intentionality and cooperative-breeding respectively), and Henrich cites his social-brain parameter while reframing the underlying mechanism in terms of cumulative cultural learning rather than Machiavellian intelligence. The parameter survives the disagreement, which strengthens rather than weakens its load-bearing role across traditions.

convergences.name: Wanting-Liking Dissociation as Proxy Hijack
convergences.statement: Motivational pursuit ('wanting', mediated by mesolimbic dopamine) is dissociable from hedonic satisfaction ('liking'), creating an open-loop vulnerability where proxy cues can activate the SEEKING system without providing the conditions that normally terminate or resolve it - established independently by Berridge & Robinson's incentive-salience neuroscience, Tinbergen's supernormal-stimuli ethology, and Panksepp's SEEKING-circuit affective neuroscience.
independent_literatures: Berridge & Robinson incentive salience / mesolimbic dopamine

convergences.name: Adverse Experience Cascading Dose-Response
convergences.statement: Chronic or developmental adverse experiences cascade across physiological systems in a dose-response manner, with effects that propagate and self-maintain across domains (metabolic, immune, neural, behavioral) - established independently by Felitti's ACE Study clinical epidemiology, McEwen's allostatic-load neuroendocrinology, and Panksepp's cross-system inhibition evidence for affective-system propagation.

convergences.name: Error Management Asymmetry in Defensive Systems
convergences.statement: Defensive systems evolved under asymmetric error costs err reliably toward over-activation (false-positive) because the cost of missing a true threat historically exceeded the cost of reacting to a non-threat - established independently by Nesse's smoke-detector principle in evolutionary psychiatry, Haselton & Buss's Error Management Theory in evolutionary social psychology, and the Öhman/Mineka fear-module visual-attention program in cognitive neuroscience.
independent_literatures: Nesse smoke detector principle evolutionary psychiatry
Öhman & Mineka fear module cognitive neuroscience

convergences.name: Allostatic Load Accumulation
convergences.statement: Chronic unresolved activation of stress-response systems produces cumulative physiological damage across multiple organ systems (allostatic load), reversible in part by environmental correction - established independently by McEwen's neuroendocrinology of allostasis and allostatic load, Felitti's ACE dose-response clinical epidemiology, and Sapolsky's primate stress physiology linking hierarchy type to cortisol and downstream pathology.
independent_literatures: McEwen allostasis and allostatic load neuroendocrinology

mechanisms.name: Threat Management
mechanisms.description: Defensive system responding to threat cues. Includes offensive mode (frustration-aggression) absorbed from old M3 RAGE.
mechanisms.eli5: Detects danger. Makes you freeze, fight, or flee.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Safety established, threat eliminated or avoided.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Modern threats - debt, emails, social media, status anxiety - can't be fought or fled. System stays on.

mechanisms.name: Reproductive Motivation
mechanisms.description: Fisher's three-system model. Independent from M2 pursuit. HPG axis, not just brain circuits.
mechanisms.eli5: Three components - desire, attraction, and pair bonding - that can work independently of each other. This system shapes much more of human behavior than reproduction itself.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Sexual fulfillment within pair bond + pair bond stability + reproductive success.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Pornography, dating apps, delayed reproduction, social media mate-value comparison.
mechanisms.proxy_gradient: 5 levels: real partner (FULL) → attenuated real / LDR (PARTIAL) → partial proxy / dating app to real meeting (MIXED) → pure proxy soft / romance fiction / parasocial (MINIMAL) → pure proxy hard / pornography / AI sexual (ZERO)

mechanisms.name: Pursuit / Exploration
mechanisms.description: General-purpose seeking system. SCOPE CHANGE from v3: sexual motivation removed (now M14). M2's resolution conditions narrow to: engagement with real-world challenges producing tangible outcomes, goal progress, skill building, resource acquisition.
mechanisms.eli5: Drives you to explore, pursue goals, and engage with the world.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Engagement with real challenges producing tangible outcomes.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Variable-reward apps fire SEEKING without resolution. Open loop.

mechanisms.name: Social Bonding
mechanisms.description: Attachment and co-regulation system. Includes grief mode (absorbed from old M9 GRIEF). Two states: security (enables broadly) and grief (suppresses broadly).
mechanisms.eli5: Forms bonds with other humans. Pain-grade distress when they're gone.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Reliable human co-regulation through physical presence. Small-scale-society evidence suggests a stable network of several intimate co-regulators (hunter-gatherer data typically shows ~5+); the critical variable is reliability, not count.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Nuclear family, isolated living, mediated contact substituting for physical presence.

mechanisms.name: Social Calibration / Play
mechanisms.description: Play system develops social skills. Absent in threatened or ill animals.
mechanisms.eli5: Learns social rules through rough-and-tumble play. Requires real playmates.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Structured activities replacing free play. Screens replacing bodies. Play deprivation.

mechanisms.name: Status Monitoring
mechanisms.description: Rank regulation. Shame, pride, submission, defeat, rank vigilance. Not optional - runs continuously.
mechanisms.eli5: Constantly monitors where you stand relative to others.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Acceptable position in ~150-person reference group. Earned prestige preferred over coerced dominance (Henrich & Gil-White distinction - needs explicit treatment).
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Global comparison via social media. Millions of "peers." Status-defeat cascade.

mechanisms.name: Controllability / Agency
mechanisms.description: vmPFC-DRN controllability circuit. Learned helplessness is the DEFAULT; agency is the override. Critical for breaking cascades.
mechanisms.eli5: Tracks whether your actions produce results. Default is passivity - agency must be learned.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Abstract work where effort doesn't visibly register. Rigid hierarchies. Bureaucratic systems.

mechanisms.name: Circadian Regulation
mechanisms.description: SCN master clock. Gates metabolism, immunity, cognition, mood. Mismatch is usually timing/quality, not duration.
mechanisms.eli5: Synchronizes every system to the light-dark cycle. Master timing architecture.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Dark-cued sleep onset, bright daylight exposure, consistent rhythm.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Electric light, blue screens, shift work, caffeine

mechanisms.name: Immune Regulation
mechanisms.description: Cytokine signaling system. Overrides voluntary intention. Hub connecting inflammation to depression via DA3 cascade.
mechanisms.eli5: Sickness behavior. Inflammation shuts everything else down to fight infection.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Chronic low-grade inflammation from diet, sedentarism, social isolation.

mechanisms.name: Care / Alloparenting
mechanisms.description: Parental and alloparental care system. Oxytocin/prolactin circuits.
mechanisms.eli5: Distributed care - the organism expects a village, not solo parenting.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: A distributed care network rather than solo or dyadic parenting. Traditional-society data typically shows multiple non-parent caregivers (~4+); the critical variable is the presence of a network.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Nuclear family without alloparental network. Parent burnout cascade.

mechanisms.name: Movement / Regulatory
mechanisms.description: Physical activity as zeitgeber, anti-inflammatory, mood regulator.
mechanisms.eli5: Movement isn't just exercise - it activates repair mechanisms throughout the organism.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Movement that's part of daily life rather than set aside as 'exercise'. Hunter-gatherers typically moved 9-15 km a day as a side effect of how they lived.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Sedentarism. Gym sessions as partial compensation.

mechanisms.name: Cooperation / Alliance
mechanisms.description: Reciprocity + norm enforcement + alliance dynamics. Absorbs old M10 (Cooperation) and M11 (Coalitional). NOTE: Tier 2 reflects epistemic honesty. No single convergence FORCES a dedicated mechanism - but four converging streams strongly support it.
mechanisms.eli5: Reciprocal exchange, cheater detection, coalitional identity.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Trusted reciprocal network with norm enforcement. No shared fiction.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Anonymous transactions, ghosting, no shared narrative.

mechanisms.name: Contamination Avoidance
mechanisms.description: Three-domain disgust (pathogen, sexual, moral). Behavioral immune system.
mechanisms.eli5: Disgust system. Pathogen avoidance extended to moral/social contamination.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Clean food, clean social environment, moral disgust contained.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Moral disgust weaponized in politics, purity spirals.

mechanisms.name: Energy Regulation
mechanisms.description: Pontzer's constrained energy model. Energy is redistributed, not burned.
mechanisms.eli5: Hunger, satiety, and constrained total energy expenditure.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Effortful, diverse, seasonal whole foods. Metabolic variety.

mechanisms.name: Touch
mechanisms.description: NOT a motivational system. Regulatory input to M3. C-tactile afferents.
mechanisms.eli5: C-tactile nerve input to bonding. Regulatory channel, not a motivation.
mechanisms.resolution_conditions: Regular physical contact with trusted others.
mechanisms.mismatch_prediction: Touch-starved modern environments. Kangaroo care evidence: 40% mortality reduction in premature infants.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Forest Troop Culture Change
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Hierarchy type (not the mere existence of hierarchy) determines pathology, and a troop's social culture is transmissible and reversible within a single generation without genetic turnover.
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Sapolsky's long-studied olive baboon troop in Kenya had its most aggressive adult males eliminated by a tuberculosis outbreak circa 1983 (the aggressive males were the ones willing to forgo morning socializing to raid a tourist-lodge garbage dump and consumed tubercular meat). The surviving troop - skewed to a 2:1 female-to-male ratio and disproportionately unaggressive, affiliative males - developed a dramatically more peaceful social style: more grooming, more sitting in contact, minimal displacement aggression, lower baseline cortisol. As new males transferred in over subsequent years (males leaving their natal troops at puberty), they were absorbed into the troop and adopted its low-aggression high-affiliation style, despite having grown up in typical aggressive troops. The culture was being transmitted horizontally without genetic change. This is a natural experiment, not a randomized intervention, but its explanatory power for the possibility of environmental correction of hierarchy-driven pathology is substantial.

empirical_demonstrations.name: The Roseto Effect
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Italian-American town had half the heart disease of neighbors - not from diet, exercise, or genetics, but from social cohesion. When the community dissolved, the protection vanished.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: 50% lower MI mortality during social cohesion; matched national average after dissolution
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Roseto, Pennsylvania was studied for 50 years (1935-1985). Despite worse risk factors - higher fat diets, similar smoking rates - the town had significantly lower myocardial infarction mortality than neighboring Bangor. The protective factor was social architecture: multigenerational households, community rituals, an egalitarian ethos that discouraged ostentatious wealth display. When younger generations adopted American individualism - separate homes, status competition, weakened ties - heart disease mortality rose to match the national average. Same genetics. Same geography. Different social inputs. Different outputs. (Egolf et al. 1992, American Journal of Public Health; Wolf & Bruhn 1993, The Roseto Story.)

empirical_demonstrations.name: Captive vs Wild Chimpanzee Abnormality
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Aversive behavioral outputs in captive primates are a function of environmental mismatch rather than defective design: 100% of captive chimps studied exhibited at least one abnormal behavior, while 0% of wild chimps did. Strongest non-human demonstration of defensive signals under mismatch (DA1).
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: 100% captive vs 0% wild show abnormal behavior
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Birkett & Newton-Fisher (2011) systematically surveyed abnormal behaviors (stereotypies, coprophagy, self-mutilation, rocking, etc.) in captive chimpanzees across accredited zoos and sanctuaries. They found these behaviors present in 100% of individuals across all studied populations. Wild chimpanzee observations find 0% prevalence of the same behaviors. The implication: the chimpanzee phenotype includes a suite of 'abnormal' behaviors only when the environment falls outside the species' expected range. The hardware works; environment is wrong.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Bucharest Early Intervention Project
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Early developmental calibration (DA7) depends on the presence of specific social input; institutional care without an alloparental network produces durable brain, attachment, and cognitive deficits that are partially reversible by foster placement but persistently worse than never-institutionalized outcomes.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: Foster care doubles IQ recovery vs institutional care
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: The Bucharest Early Intervention Project (Zeanah, Nelson, Fox and colleagues) randomized institutionalized Romanian children to continued institutional care vs high-quality foster care and compared both groups to never-institutionalized community children. Across neurodevelopmental, attachment, cognitive, and psychiatric outcomes, the foster-care cohort showed substantial recovery (~2× IQ improvement, reduced disorganized attachment, reduced psychopathology) compared to the institutional-care cohort, with earlier placement predicting better outcomes. But neither ever caught up fully to the never-institutionalized community controls, confirming developmental calibration windows and the severity of the mismatch between institutional care and the evolved expectable environment.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Roseto Effect
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Intense social cohesion can override standard cardiovascular risk factors to produce dramatically lower heart disease mortality - and the effect erodes within a generation once cohesion breaks down, confirming the causal role of the social input rather than any genetic or dietary confounder.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: ~50% lower heart disease mortality during cohesion era
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Roseto, Pennsylvania was a small Italian-American immigrant community with unusually strong intergenerational family structures, tight social cohesion, and community rituals through the 1950s-60s. Stewart Wolf and colleagues documented roughly half the heart disease mortality of surrounding communities despite comparable standard risk factors (diet, smoking, occupational exposures). As the younger generation assimilated into mainstream American individualism across the 1970s-80s, the social cohesion eroded and the mortality advantage disappeared on schedule. The Roseto effect is a natural experiment for DA2/DC1: strong social scaffolding can suppress the cardiovascular consequences of allostatic load, and its removal reveals the baseline.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Ilardi TLC Protocol for Depression
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Treating depression by restoring resolution conditions across multiple mechanisms simultaneously (exercise, omega-3, sleep, sunlight, anti-rumination, social connection) produces remission rates comparable to or exceeding SSRI monotherapy - consistent with depression-as-mismatch-signal rather than depression-as-broken-brain.
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Stephen Ilardi's Therapeutic Lifestyle Change protocol is a six-lever intervention targeting M10 (movement), M7 (sleep and sunlight), M3 (social connection), M8/M13 (omega-3 / anti-inflammation), and cognitive anti-rumination. In his clinical trials and subsequent implementations, remission rates in moderate-to-severe depression have reached ~75%, comparable to or exceeding typical SSRI monotherapy rates (~45% remission). The protocol is consistent with Cor's prediction that many depression presentations are M1/M3/M5/M6 defensive-signal cascades resolvable by restoring the resolution conditions for the affected mechanisms.

empirical_demonstrations.name: SMILES Trial: Diet and Depression
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: A Mediterranean-style dietary intervention produces clinically meaningful remission from moderate-to-severe depression at ~4× the rate of social-support control over 12 weeks, supporting the causal role of M13 (energy regulation / diet quality) and M8 (inflammation) in depression onset/maintenance rather than treating diet as a correlate.
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Jacka et al. 2017 (BMC Medicine) conducted the SMILES randomized controlled trial, comparing a Mediterranean-style dietary intervention to a befriending/social-support control in adults with moderate-to-severe major depressive disorder. At 12 weeks, 32% of the dietary intervention group met remission criteria vs 8% of the control group. NNT ≈ 4. The effect is unusually large for a dietary intervention in a psychiatric population and has subsequently been partially replicated (HELFIMED and follow-up trials). SMILES grounds a causal rather than merely correlational reading of diet-depression links and supports the M13/M8/DA3 cascade.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Exercise as Depression Treatment
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Aerobic exercise at moderate intensity produces depression symptom reduction comparable to SSRI pharmacotherapy in head-to-head randomized trials and meta-analyses, supporting M10 (movement/regulatory) as a central mechanism in depression pathophysiology rather than a general-health adjunct.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: Cohen's d ~0.8; non-inferior to SSRI in RCTs
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Multiple meta-analyses (Schuch et al. 2016 J Psychiatr Res and subsequent updates) pooling dozens of randomized trials find moderate-intensity aerobic exercise produces Cohen's d ~0.8 reduction in depression severity, comparable to SSRI effect sizes in the same literatures. Several head-to-head trials (Blumenthal et al. and follow-ups) show no significant difference between exercise monotherapy and sertraline at 16 weeks, with exercise showing better long-term relapse prevention. Exercise is not a correlate or adjunct; it is a primary intervention in its own right, consistent with M10's role in the depression cascade.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Chronotherapy in Depression
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Total sleep deprivation combined with morning bright light and sleep-phase advance produces 60%+ response rates in bipolar depression within days - orders of magnitude faster than pharmacotherapy - by targeting M7 (circadian regulation) directly. Demonstrates M7 as a cascade entry point.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: ~60% bipolar depression response within first week
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Wirz-Justice, Benedetti, and colleagues have developed chronotherapeutic protocols for treatment-resistant bipolar depression combining one night of total sleep deprivation (to rapidly shift circadian phase), morning bright light therapy (to re-entrain M7), and sleep-phase advance (to lock in the new timing). Response rates of 60%+ within the first week are consistent across multiple European trials, compared with ~30% for pharmacotherapy alone over the same interval. The protocol works because it targets the timing architecture that many depression cascades propagate through.

empirical_demonstrations.name: Kangaroo Mother Care
empirical_demonstrations.what_it_demonstrates: Skin-to-skin contact between premature infants and caregivers reduces mortality, improves physiological regulation, and produces long-term developmental benefits - demonstrating R1 (C-tactile touch as regulatory input to M3) as architecture rather than comfort measure, with effects that persist two decades.
empirical_demonstrations.primary_metric_display: ~40% neonatal mortality reduction; benefits at 20 years
empirical_demonstrations.narrative: Charpak et al. developed and studied Kangaroo Mother Care in Bogotá, Colombia for low-birth-weight infants: continuous skin-to-skin contact with the mother (or other caregiver) 24 hours a day, replacing or supplementing incubator care. In low-resource settings the intervention reduces neonatal mortality by ~40% compared to standard incubator care; in high-resource settings it produces improved physiological regulation (thermoregulation, heart rate variability, weight gain) and long-term cognitive and behavioral benefits demonstrated at 20-year follow-up (Charpak et al. 2017 Pediatrics). Feldman's longitudinal work confirms the effects on stress reactivity and executive function into adolescence. Kangaroo care is the strongest evidence that R1 (C-tactile input) is a genuine regulatory channel with architecture-level consequences, not a comfort measure.

challenges.content: The EAAT paradox: if cognitive faculties are unreliable products of selection, the EAAT itself cannot be trusted (self-defeating). But dismissing EAAT requires assuming fitness-truth correlation (veridical perceptual congruence) which contemporary evolutionary biology cannot establish. Evolution is not progressive: gnats may be fitter than mammals yet perceive less veridically. Result: EAAT is both tenable and untenable simultaneously.
challenges.caveats: Philosophical challenge to the Evaluativist-Analogical-Axiomatic Theory frame. Presents a self-defeating paradox in the specific EAAT formulation; does not invalidate the broader mismatch thesis.
challenges.title: Darwin's "Horrid" Doubt Revisited: A Critical Reassessment of Evolutionary Arguments against Truth

challenges.content: Three nationally representative time-use-diary studies (N=17,247; Ireland, US, UK) found little evidence for substantial negative associations between digital-screen engagement and adolescent well-being, including screen time before bedtime. Time-use diaries are more accurate than self-report, and the effect sizes were negligible. This challenges the dominant narrative that screen time per se is harmful.
challenges.caveats: Time-use diary data from Orben & Przybylski and adjacent meta-analyses show small effects for social-media-use / wellbeing correlations, on the order of r ≈ 0.05. Challenges the largest versions of the social-media-harm thesis without invalidating mechanism-level claims.
challenges.title: Screens, Teens, and Psychological Well-Being: Evidence From Three Time-Use-Diary Studies.

challenges.content: Meta-analysis of social media use and depressive symptoms among adolescents (11-18 years) found a small but significant positive correlation (k=12, r=0.11, p<.01) with high heterogeneity (I2=95.22%). The small overall effect size and extreme heterogeneity suggest that overall use measures mask highly variable effects depending on type of use, platform, and individual vulnerability.
challenges.caveats: Meta-analytic finding of a small (r ≈ 0.11) positive correlation between adolescent social-media use and depressive symptoms, with extreme heterogeneity. The small overall effect and high variance indicate that aggregate-use measures hide substantial differences by platform, type of use, and individual vulnerability.
challenges.title: A meta-analysis of the association between adolescent social media use and depressive symptoms.
challenges.authors: Ivie EJ, Pettitt A, Moses LJ, Allen NB
challenges.source: Ivie EJ, Pettitt A, Moses LJ, Allen NB

challenges.content: Largest meta-analysis to date on social media and adolescent internalizing symptoms (143 studies, N=1,094,890, 886 effect sizes). Found small but significant meta-correlations: time spent on social media and internalizing symptoms r=0.08 (clinical samples) and similar in community samples. User engagement showed r=0.12 in clinical samples. Only 11% of studies examined clinical populations, revealing a critical gap: research focuses on general population while public concern is about clinical-level symptoms.
challenges.caveats: Large meta-analysis (approx. N = 1 million) finds small effects with high heterogeneity for the social-media-to-internalizing-symptoms link. Reports the effect-size reality check that mechanism-level claims must respect, and highlights the gap between general-population research and clinical-sample concerns.
challenges.title: Social Media Use and Internalizing Symptoms in Clinical and Community Adolescent Samples: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
challenges.authors: Fassi L, Thomas K, Parry DA, Leyland-Craggs A, Ford TJ, Orben A
challenges.source: Fassi L, Thomas K, Parry DA, Leyland-Craggs A, Ford TJ, Orben A

challenges.content: Meta-analysis of 50 studies (published and unpublished) testing whether incidental disgust amplifies moral judgment. Raw effect: d=0.11 (small), strongest for gustatory/olfactory induction. After correcting for publication bias, the effect disappears entirely (d=-0.01). Prevalent confounds mean d=0.11 is an upper bound. The finding that the incidental disgust amplification effect may be artifactual challenges strong claims about emotion causally driving moral judgment and suggests the Haidt and Wheatley 2005 disgust-priming finding may not generalize robustly.
challenges.caveats: Publication-bias-corrected meta-analysis of the disgust-moral-judgment effect yields effect size ≈ 0. Challenges the strong version of the disgust-amplifies-moral-judgment literature, including the generalizability of the Haidt & Wheatley 2005 disgust-priming finding.
challenges.title: Does Incidental Disgust Amplify Moral Judgment? A Meta-Analytic Review of Experimental Evidence

gaps.name: Challenge layer depth
gaps.what_is_missing: The current challenge layer holds 5 challenge extractions (EAAT philosophical critique, social-media-effect-size meta-analyses, Landy disgust-moral meta-analysis). Dedicated challenges from Active Inference (Friston) as a competing framework, substance-specific addiction neuroscience (Volkow/Kreek), and cultural-construction critiques of specific mechanism claims beyond Barrett are not yet in the corpus.
gaps.why_it_matters: A credible framework must engage its strongest critics. The current challenge layer is thin and leans on methodological critiques rather than framework-level competitors like Active Inference or RDoC.
gaps.current_approach: The current challenge layer is treated as sufficient for initial framing. A dedicated challenge-layer expansion is a priority for the next round of work, covering Active Inference, addiction neuroscience, and RDoC comparisons.

gaps.name: M14 cross-cultural variation evidence
gaps.what_is_missing: M14 is sex-differentiated, with distinct female (VMH-centered) and male (POA-centered) neural architectures carrying different emphasis on commitment cues versus variety cues. Cross-cultural tests of these specific claims are thin, and the ovulatory-shift literature in particular has an active replication debate.
gaps.why_it_matters: M14 is the most culturally contested mechanism and the one most likely to be challenged on grounds of cultural construction. A weak empirical cross-cultural base makes the mechanism vulnerable to legitimate challenge even though the core neuroarchitecture is well-supported.
gaps.current_approach: Haselton/Buss Error Management Theory and Buss sexual strategies work are the current evidence base. Ovulatory-shift-specific claims are explicitly flagged as contested in extraction caveats to preserve the nuance.

gaps.name: M6 human neurobiology replication
gaps.what_is_missing: The Maier & Seligman 2016 reformulation of learned helplessness (default passivity with vmPFC-DRN override) is well-supported in rodent models, but the human neurobiological translation - fMRI studies of controllability in humans - is thinner. Karasek's decision-latitude work provides strong human-level evidence but at a different level of analysis.
gaps.why_it_matters: M6 is critical as a cascade-break point in Cor's applied framework (A2 clinical practice, A3 environment design, A6 work regulation). Strong rodent evidence combined with weaker human-specific neural evidence leaves the mechanism vulnerable to challenge at the human-specificity level.
gaps.current_approach: The rodent-level circuit claims are treated as settled; the human-level circuit mapping is treated as well-supported rather than definitively established. The distinction is preserved in the mechanism description.

gaps.name: Bridge Thesis BT1 empirical discriminator
gaps.what_is_missing: BT1 (Panksepp-Barrett Resolution) is defended conceptually - subcortical affective circuits are real, and folk emotion categories are constructed overlays on top of them. What is missing is a crisp empirical discriminator test that would adjudicate specific cases where the Panksepp and Barrett frameworks make divergent predictions.
gaps.why_it_matters: Bridge theses are load-bearing for Cor's epistemic credibility. If a bridge is only conceptually defensible and cannot be tested against a concrete prediction, critics can characterize the resolution as rhetorical rather than substantive.
gaps.current_approach: BT1 is presented as a level-of-analysis distinction: Panksepp's cross-species deep-brain-stimulation results ground the primary-process claim; Barrett's cross-cultural folk-category variation grounds the tertiary-process claim. The discriminator gap is explicitly acknowledged in the bridge-thesis narrative.

gaps.name: Cultural-matched vs ancestrally-matched distinction
gaps.what_is_missing: Boyd & Richerson's gene-culture coevolution constraint requires that 'matched' include cultural scaffolding, not just ancestral surface forms. But operationalizing which cultural configurations count as matched versus mismatched requires a criterion that does not collapse into either ancestral essentialism (only hunter-gatherer life counts) or cultural relativism (everything counts).
gaps.why_it_matters: Without this distinction sharpened, P3 (Systematic Mismatch) can be attacked as either too permissive (all cultures are matched by definition) or too restrictive (only hunter-gatherer configurations count).
gaps.current_approach: The constraint is treated as 'matched must lie within the envelope of culturally-scaffolded human variation historically observed.' The criterion is acknowledged to be not fully sharp and flagged for sharper operationalization in future theoretical work.

gaps.name: M14 proxy gradient clinical validation
gaps.what_is_missing: The five-level M14 proxy gradient (real partner → attenuated real → partial proxy → pure proxy soft → pure proxy hard) is architecturally derived but lacks clinical validation metrics. Which clinical presentations map to which proxy level, and what are the dose-response clinical consequences of sustained use at each level, remain open questions.
gaps.why_it_matters: A1 AI alignment risk analysis and A2 clinical practice both depend on being able to locate specific proxy products on the gradient with some reliability.
gaps.current_approach: The gradient is used as a theoretical organizing device. Clinical validation is flagged as a priority research program for A2 clinical applications.

gaps.name: Tier 3 regulatory-input candidates beyond R1
gaps.what_is_missing: R1 (Touch) is currently the only Tier 3 regulatory input in the inventory. Other candidate regulatory inputs deserve architectural consideration: thermoregulation signaling to M2/M6, gut-brain axis signaling to M3/M8, hydration signaling, and proprioceptive/vestibular input regulation.
gaps.why_it_matters: R1's inclusion establishes the Tier 3 category - but leaving it as a set of one invites scope questions about why touch and not other regulatory inputs are architecturally load-bearing.
gaps.current_approach: R1 is treated as the template for the Tier 3 category. Additional regulatory inputs are candidates for future architectural work but are not added retroactively without explicit review.

gaps.name: DC2 empirical depth beyond Schüll and Moss
gaps.what_is_missing: Dedicated empirical work on Haugen's Facebook Files and on companion AI harm research. One cited reference (Muldoon & Park) has not been located in accessible research databases; the underlying finding needs either identification or replacement with equivalent sourced work.
gaps.why_it_matters: DC2 (Market Proxy Exploitation) grounds A1 AI alignment and A6 platform regulation. It currently rests on Schüll (gambling) and Moss (food), which is narrow. Companion AI is the highest-risk contemporary case and does not yet have primary-source grounding in Cor.
gaps.current_approach: Flagged as pending in DC2's derivation and in A1's AI alignment risk-pattern analysis. The Muldoon & Park reference is treated as unverified and its claims are not attributed or cited until the underlying research is identified or replaced.

gaps.name: Matched-state measurement protocol
gaps.what_is_missing: Cor specifies resolution conditions per mechanism (for example M3: reliable intimate co-regulators typically ~5+; M10: varied daily movement typically ~9-15km/day; M7: dark-cued sleep onset), but operationalizing 'matched' as a measurable state across all 14 mechanisms - with inter-rater and test-retest reliability - is unfinished.
gaps.why_it_matters: The measurement-protocol gap limits A4 personal assessment from becoming a validated instrument and blocks quantitative A6 policy evaluation against mechanism resolution conditions.
gaps.current_approach: A public-facing tool built on the framework will prototype the measurement protocol for A4 individual-level audits. Formal psychometric validation is independent research downstream of the core framework.

gaps.name: AI alignment matched/mismatched evaluation axis operationalization
gaps.what_is_missing: A1 proposes 'matched / mismatched' as a fourth evaluation axis alongside helpful, harmless, and honest. What is missing is a concrete set of test cases - what a 'matched' response to a specific user query looks like versus a 'mismatched' one - scored in a way that a red-team or evaluation harness can apply systematically.
gaps.why_it_matters: A1's matched/mismatched evaluation proposal is currently a framework, not an evaluation instrument. Without concrete scorable test cases, the matched/mismatched axis is a conceptual contribution to alignment evaluation rather than a working protocol that labs can run against model outputs.
gaps.current_approach: A1's evaluation-criteria field carries the resolution-check and proxy-gradient tests as conceptual criteria. Operational red-team-scorable test cases are the next step of the alignment evaluation work.
